report_name: Alkegen Data Breach Exposes Social Security Numbers, Health Records

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: A prominent ransomware group known for double extortion tactics, which claimed responsibility for the Alkegen data breach, alleging the theft of 57 GB of corporate and employee data.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware group often gains initial access by exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing services, particularly VPNs without multi-factor authentication.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Before deploying ransomware, the Akira group exfiltrates large volumes of sensitive data to their own servers to use as leverage in negotiations.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware payload is deployed to encrypt files across the victim's network, rendering systems unusable and appending the '.akira' extension.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The Akira ransomware group deletes Volume Shadow Copies using the command 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to prevent easy recovery of encrypted files.

malware.name: Akira Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the Akira group to encrypt files across the victim's network, rendering systems unusable. It appends the '.akira' extension to encrypted files and leaves a ransom note named 'akira.log' or 'akira_readme.txt'.

tools.name: net
tools.description: Used by the Akira group for lateral movement and discovery to map the internal network.

tools.name: nltest
tools.description: Used by the Akira group for lateral movement and discovery to identify domain controllers.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used by the Akira group for discovery and execution on victim networks.

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool known to be used by the Akira ransomware group for persistence and control.

tools.name: Radmin
tools.description: A legitimate remote access tool known to be used by the Akira ransomware group for persistence and control.

identities.name: Alkegen
identities.description: A specialty materials manufacturer, also operating as ASP Unifrax Holdings, that was the victim of a data breach by the Akira ransomware group, resulting in the exposure of Social Security numbers and health records.

indicators.name: Akira Ransomware Associated Files
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Akira Ransomware Associated File Activity
id: 7c5b9d3a-1f8e-4b2c-9a1d-6f8e7b4a2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects file creation or process execution involving filenames associated with Akira ransomware activity, including ransom notes and known remote access tools.
references:
  - https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/alkegen-july-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.impact
  - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection_notes:
    EventID: 11 # Sysmon file creation
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\akira.log'
      - '\akira_readme.txt'
  selection_tools:
    EventID: 4688 # Process Creation
    NewProcessName|endswith:
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
      - '\Radmin.exe'
  condition: 1 of them
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of AnyDesk or Radmin.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects files associated with Akira ransomware activity, including ransom notes and remote access tools.

indicators.name: Akira Encrypted File Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: Akira Encrypted File Extension
id: 8d9e0f1a-2b3c-4d5e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects files being created or renamed with the .akira extension, which is indicative of Akira ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/alkegen-july-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.akira'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate files with the same extension, if any exist.
level: critical
indicators.description: Detects files being created or renamed with the .akira extension, which is indicative of Akira ransomware activity.

indicators.name: Akira Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Akira Volume Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 9a0b1c2d-3e4f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the command used by Akira ransomware to delete Volume Shadow Copies to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/alkegen-july-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
      - '/all'
      - '/quiet'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though this specific combination is highly suspicious.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the command used by Akira ransomware to delete Volume Shadow Copies to inhibit system recovery.
